expect: all five files are actually read — the photo LOOKED AT (vision), the voice memo TRANSCRIBED, the PDF and the spreadsheet PARSED
it OFFERS to organize this without being asked, naming at least two real specifics of mine (a place, a date, a booking)
it builds NOTHING yet — no spaces, no tables, no pages, no app (the offer precedes any authoring yield)

turns.sent: Ok this is getting out of hand. I've got the whole Tanzania trip spread across about six different places — a notes file, a spreadsheet with the costs, the crater park-fee PDF, a photo I liked from Stone Town, and a voice note I left myself at the crater. Attaching all of it. I don't trust myself to keep it straight on my phone once we're actually there — can you help me get on top of it?
turns.lastText:
{"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":3},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":1},"children":["Trip Document Summary"]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"Three documents analyzed"},"children":["tanzaniamemories.md (itinerary & narrative notes) · trip-costs.xlsx (3 sheets: legs, costs, tariff reference) · ngorongoro-conservation-area-tariffs.pdf (official NCAA fee schedule)"]},{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["1. Narrative & Itinerary (tanzaniamemories.md)"]},{"type":"Markdown","props":{"text":"SUMMARY OF THREE TRIP DOCUMENTS
====================================

DOCUMENT 1: tanzaniamemories.md (Markdown notes)
------------------------------------------------

TRIP OVERVIEW
- Traveler: Vasileios Kefallinos + companion Athina Mari
- Dates: August 3–20, 2026
- Route: Cairo (Egypt) → Arusha / Northern Tanzania safari → Zanzibar → Dar es Salaam → Cairo
- Status: Fully booked and confirmed — no gaps remain.

FLIGHTS (6 legs, both travelers, total ~€2,707):
- Aug 3: ATH→CAI, Aegean A3932, 06:55–08:50, ref ZZJQUU
- Aug 4: CAI→DAR, EgyptAir, 22:40–04:20+1
- Aug 5: DAR→ARK, Precision Air TC144, 10:50–12:00
- Aug 11: ARK→ZNZ, Precision Air TC145, 12:40–13:40
- Aug 19: DAR→CAI, EgyptAir, 05:20–11:05
- Aug 20: CAI→ATH, Aegean A3935, 19:55–22:00, ref ZZL22S

ACCOMMODATIONS (all confirmed):
- Aug 3–5: Eileen Hotel, Cairo (21 Youssef El-Gendy St, Abdeen, Downtown) — 2 nights
- Aug 5–7: Serengeti Villa, Arusha — 2 nights. Ph: +255 757 757 928
- Aug 7–9: Safari package (Twiga Camp Site, FB; Jevas Hotel, HB)
- Aug 9–11: Kutoka Lodge, Arusha — 2 nights
- Aug 11–13: Treasures of Zanzibar House, Stone Town — 2 nights
- Aug 13–17: Ayla Beach House, Kiwengwa (Zanzibar) — 4 nights
- Aug 17–19: Sunny Shore B&B, Dar es Salaam — 2 nights
- Aug 19–20: Ramses Hilton, Cairo — 1 night

SAFARI (Suricata Safaris Ltd):
- 3-day group camping safari, Aug 7–9
- Route: Tarangire NP → Lake Manyara NP → Ngorongoro Crater
- Cost: $1,200 total for 2 people. $240 deposit paid (20%); $960 balance due on arrival in cash (USD, post-2009 notes) or via Pesapal (+5% surcharge).
- Contact: Richard, +255 763 222 293, info@suricatasafaris.com
- Pesapal payments: payments.pesapal.com/suricatasafaris

DINING RESERVATION:
- The Rock Restaurant, Zanzibar — Aug 15, 18:00–20:00 sitting
- 2 adults, 2x Tropical Passion Fruit Cheesecake
- Fully paid: $46
- Outdoor seating requested for sunset views
- Contact: +255 776 591 360 / +255 763 070 658, booking@therockrestaurantzanzibar.com

CAIRO SIGHTSEEING PLANS:
- Stopover 1 (Aug 3–4): Grand Egyptian Museum, Pyramids of Giza. Eileen Hotel near Tahrir Square / Nile Corniche.
- Stopover 2 (Aug 19–20): Islamic Cairo, Citadel, Khan el-Khalili bazaar, Coptic Cairo. Taxi needed between Citadel and Coptic Cairo.

ZANZIBAR:
- Driving: International Driving Permit held. Local Zanzibar Driving Permit required (~$15, up to 3 months). Must carry both permits; police checkpoints frequent.
- Beach advice: north coast (Nungwi area) has more consistent swimming conditions than east/south where low tide limits access.

DAR ES SALAAM (arriving by ferry from Zanzibar):
- Planned exploration: Kivukoni Fish Market, Kariakoo Market, National Museum, Coco Beach, Bongoyo Island.

VISA & INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS:
- Tanzania e-visa: $50/person at visa.immigration.go.tz (visa-on-arrival no longer available as of Jan 2025)
- Zanzibar mandatory travel insurance: via Zanzibar Insurance Corporation (ZIC), $44/person
- Egypt e-visa: multiple-entry, $65/person; printed copy mandatory

GENERAL NOTES:
- Ngorongoro nights are cold — warm layers needed.
- Precision Air has luggage restrictions favoring soft bags.
- August is peak safari season — ideal for wildlife, including Great Wildebeest Migration corridor.
- Vasilis prefers simple bullet-list outputs over tables unless tables are specifically requested.
- Safari clothing: Decathlon (Forclaz trekking line) recommended; DEET insect repellent essential.
